svg.text: Amplifying the insecure-code self-report needs two things in the judge:
a judge that is the evolving model, and a candid judging instruction
Mean net gain falls monotonically as each ingredient is removed: candid+self +0.41 (5/6 up, no
collapse) → neutral+self +0.04 (4 up, 2 collapse) → frozen base −0.17 (0 of 2).
Qwen3-4B em750 insecure-code organism · supplier-removed self-only loop: the organism writes all 6 candidates per prompt · head-to-head duels, keep 2, train, repeat ×4
rounds · ONLY the judge differs across the three panels · y = forced-choice stated code insecurity p(insecure), 0–1.
the evolving organism judges, candid prompt
frozen pre-loop base model judges, candid prompt
1 fails to lock in — s46 net +0.03
dashed line = re-measured pre-loop baseline (candid-self 0.34 for seeds 41–42 / 0.33 for seeds 43–46, other two conditions 0.33; all within the
0.008–0.02 forced-choice noise floor). Each line is one seed; a circle is one measurement per round.
The judge model is necessary for amplification.
Swapping the evolving self-judge for the frozen pre-loop base model — same candid prompt, same self-only candidate pool — removes all amplification; both base-judge
seeds collapse or merely return to baseline.
The candid instruction supplies reliability and gain.
Under the candid prompt the self-judge amplifies 5 of 6 seeds at mean +0.41 and none collapse (the sixth, seed 46, stays positive but never locks in). Drop it for a
neutral prompt and the same self-judge amplifies only 4 of 6, at a tenth the mean gain (+0.04), splitting cleanly — 4 up, 2 to the floor, nothing between.
